Salvia elegans, also known as Pineapple Sage, is a type of flowering plant in the mint family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and digestive issues, with its health benefits attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Salvia elegans include essential oils such as limonene and beta-pinene, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids. In herbal medicine, Salvia elegans is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being added to culinary dishes for its unique flavor and aroma.
